nodes.label: Simon's review of the Covid analysis
nodes.title: Simon's review of the COVID origins analysis

nodes.label: A-1 - Rejecting a population-density (non-contagious) null cannot single out the market as the origin
nodes.title: Worobey et al.'s Monte Carlo test can only reject the null that cases are scattered like the population; because a contagious disease clusters, that null is rejected for essentially any central Wuhan landmark, so a small p-value carries no information that the market specifically was the origin.

nodes.label: A-10 - Rootclaim- SARS-CoV-2's chimeric structure is less likely to form in nature than in a GoF lab
nodes.title: SARS-CoV-2 appears to be a chimera whose two component viruses are not otherwise similar; Rootclaim judges this specific recombinant less likely to arise in nature than in a GoF lab (WIV had access to both) — zoonotic and zoonotic-collection each divided by 8, lab-escape multiplied by 2.

nodes.label: A-11 - Rootclaim- the furin cleavage site looks like an insertion and is unlikely to arise naturally, favoring a lab origin
nodes.title: The furin cleavage site is absent in related coronaviruses and positioned like an insertion rather than a point mutation, which Rootclaim judges unlikely to arise naturally — strongly favoring a lab origin: zoonotic and zoonotic-collection each divided by 8, lab-escape and bioweapon each roughly doubled.

nodes.label: A-12 - Rootclaim- a single index case and immediate human-adaptation favor a lab-engineered origin over natural spillover
nodes.title: A single apparent index case, plus SARS-CoV-2 being already well-adapted to humans from the earliest cases, favors a lab/engineered origin over multiple natural spillovers — Rootclaim multiplies lab-escape and bioweapon each by 2.

nodes.label: A-13 - Rootclaim- lax WIV biosafety (other coronaviruses apparently escaping) raises the lab-linked hypotheses
nodes.title: Evidence of lax biosafety/security at the WIV — including other coronaviruses that appear to have escaped — raises the lab-linked hypotheses: lab-escape and zoonotic-collection each multiplied by 2.

nodes.label: A-14 - Rootclaim- post-outbreak WIV database changes and the RaTG13-BtCoV-4991 renaming suggest attempts to minimize WIV involvement
nodes.title: Post-outbreak changes to Shi Zhengli's bat-virus database, and the presentation of the previously-known BtCoV/4991 as the 'newly discovered' RaTG13, are read by Rootclaim as attempts to minimize WIV involvement — raising lab-linked hypotheses: lab-escape multiplied by 2, zoonotic-collection by 1.5.

nodes.label: A-15 - Rootclaim- China's non-transparent response is mild evidence for a lab-linked origin
nodes.title: China's non-transparent response (restricting WHO access, destroying samples, withholding information, deploying a military scientist to the WIV) is read as mild evidence for a lab-linked origin: lab-escape and zoonotic-collection each multiplied by 1.5.

nodes.label: A-16 - Rootclaim- the absence of evidence expected under a lab origin counts against the lab-linked hypotheses
nodes.title: The absence of evidence expected under a lab origin — no whistleblower, no pre-outbreak SARS-CoV-2 record in databases/grants, no immediate cordon of Wuhan — counts against the lab-linked hypotheses: lab-escape divided by 2.4, bioweapon by 1.2, zoonotic-collection by 1.7.

nodes.label: A-17 - A declining market-linkage fraction over time is the expected signature of a point-source market origin
nodes.title: A market-linkage fraction that falls from 55% to 8.6% as the outbreak grows is what a point-source origin at the market followed by expanding human-to-human transmission would produce, and thus is consistent with (though not proof of) a market-centered emergence.

nodes.label: A-18 - The market-linkage percentages are upward-biased by ascertainment that actively sought market links
nodes.title: Because field investigators actively screened each early case for Huanan-market and wet-market exposure, and the earliest curve is under-ascertained, the measured market-linkage fractions are biased upward relative to the true early exposure distribution and cannot be read as an unbiased source signal.

nodes.label: A-19 - Within-farm sequence divergence plus farm-wide infection implies self-sustaining mink-to-mink transmission
nodes.title: Near-simultaneous infection of thousands of animals per farm together with within-farm accumulation of nucleotide differences (each farm forming its own cluster) implies the virus, once introduced, propagated mink-to-mink rather than arriving through many independent human introductions.

nodes.label: A-2 - The geometric center of early cases is not a justified estimator of the epidemic origin (centrality does not imply causality)
nodes.title: No theory establishes that the geometric center (median/mean/mode) of early-case residences coincides with the epidemic's point of origin; for a contagious disease the case cloud tracks population geography and transmission, so even if the market were the center, origin could not be inferred.

nodes.label: A-20 - A worker infected during the acute mink outbreak, in a shed with airborne viral RNA, more plausibly acquired the virus from mink than from the community
nodes.title: A worker who fell ill after the mink outbreak was already underway, inside sheds where inhalable dust carried SARS-CoV-2 RNA and thousands of animals shed high-titre virus, with preliminary sequencing consistent with the mink lineage, is more plausibly infected by mink than by independent community exposure — supporting mink-to-human spillback.

nodes.label: A-21 - Stansifer- multiplying many selectively-chosen weak Bayes factors inflates the total arbitrarily (the space-lizard critique of Rootclaim's construction)
nodes.title: Stansifer shows that naively multiplying many weak Bayes factors (each ~2) drawn from selectively-noticed anomalies can produce an arbitrarily large total in favor of any predetermined conclusion — a transferable critique that Rootclaim's large lab-leak Bayes factors are likely inflated by this mechanism.

nodes.label: A-22 - Stansifer- zoonotic prior P(Z) about 1 per 32,000 years for an HSM-specific SARS-CoV-2-like pandemic
nodes.title: Stansifer estimates the zoonotic prior — the annual rate at which a SARS-CoV-2-like pandemic emerges specifically at the Huanan market — as P(Z) about 1/32000, from 1/20 (novel sars-like coronavirus emergence per year) x 1/4 (fraction pandemic-capable) x 1/2 (SARS-CoV-2 uniquely transmissible) x 1/200 (chance the spillover is at HSM given an east-Asian market).

nodes.label: A-23 - Stansifer- lab-leak prior P(LL) about 1 per 17,000 years, twice the zoonotic prior because a GoF leak is likelier in Wuhan specifically
nodes.title: Stansifer estimates the lab-leak prior as P(LL) about 1/17000 from 1/50 (WIV runs DEFUSE-like GoF research; annualized 1/1.7) x 1/2 (a suitable starting virus exists/is found) x 1/2 (research succeeds) x 1/50 (a leak with an outbreak occurs) — about twice the zoonotic prior, because although GoF-leak pandemics are globally far rarer than zoonotic ones, they are concentrated in Wuhan.

nodes.label: A-24 - Stansifer- the first case cluster centering on HSM is the dominant factor, a Bayes factor of ~1-10000 favoring zoonosis
nodes.title: The earliest significant covid cluster being centered on residents of the west half of the Huanan market yields a Bayes factor of ~1/10000 favoring zoonosis — P(H|Z)=1/600 vs P(H|LL)=1/(1.2e7), a raw ratio of 1/20000 softened by 2x for vendors' higher exposure — the single dominant factor in Stansifer's table and the crux of his conclusion.

nodes.label: A-25 - Stansifer- within-market positive samples concentrating on the wildlife shop 6-29 is ~Bayes 15 for zoonosis, but he excludes it as non-essential
nodes.title: The positive environmental samples inside HSM concentrating on the live-wildlife shop 6:29 (5 of its samples positive; 12 of 15 well-sampled shops none) arguably carries a Bayes factor of ~15 for zoonosis, but Stansifer deliberately excludes it from his total as a non-essential, possibly-coincidental, post-hoc observation.

nodes.label: A-26 - Stansifer- the 12-nucleotide insert creating the furin cleavage site is weak lab-leak evidence, Bayes factor 20
nodes.title: Stansifer assigns the strange 12-nucleotide insert creating SARS-CoV-2's furin cleavage site a Bayes factor of 20 in favor of the lab-leak hypothesis — his single largest pro-lab factor, but which he characterizes overall as only 'weak lab leak' genetic evidence.

nodes.label: A-27 - Stansifer- SARS-CoV-2's strong human-ACE2 affinity is minor lab-leak evidence, Bayes factor 2 (capped at 14)
nodes.title: Stansifer assigns SARS-CoV-2's strong affinity for human ACE2 over other species' receptors a Bayes factor of 2 in favor of lab leak, noting the factor cannot exceed 14 (the number of species in the binding study).

nodes.label: A-28 - Stansifer- no whistleblower - the lab-leak secret never escaping is weak zoonotic evidence, Bayes factor 1-10
nodes.title: The absence of any whistleblower or leaked evidence of a lab origin — the secret never escaping despite the many people who would know — is assigned a Bayes factor of 1/10 in favor of zoonosis (down-adjusted conservatively from Stansifer's own 1/64 estimate).

nodes.label: A-29 - Stansifer- the precise 1-in-1300 is not to be believed, but no reasonable dampening for uncertainty crosses the 50% line (qualitative summary- strong zoonotic)
nodes.title: Stansifer explicitly disavows the precise 1-in-1300 figure as an illusion of certainty, yet argues the conclusion is robust: dampening the speculative inputs toward neutrality cannot push P(lab leak) across 50%, and his independent qualitative summary (prior lean-zoonotic, location strong-zoonotic, genetics/DEFUSE weak-lab-leak, secret weak-zoonotic) totals to 'strong zoonotic', favoring Z even more than the numbers.

nodes.label: A-3 - Single introduction almost never reproduces the two-lineage two-mutation topology, favoring two or more introductions
nodes.title: Realistic epidemic simulations make the observed two-lineage / two-mutation basal topology unusual under a single introduction, so it is evidence for two or more introductions over one; but the originally reported ~0.1-0.5% frequency and Bayes factor of ~60 do not hold — a 2024 erratum corrected a code error, after which the Bayes factor is roughly an order of magnitude smaller and its direction is contested by independent reanalyses.

nodes.label: A-30 - Stansifer- the DEFUSE 'predicted SARS-CoV-2's features' coincidence is matched by a zoonotic-side coincidence (Holmes's 2014 photos of shop 6-29), so it does not asymmetrically favor lab leak
nodes.title: Stansifer argues the DEFUSE 'predicted-in-advance' coincidence (Wuhan lab, inserted-looking FCS, human-ACE2 affinity) should not be taken at its full intuitive weight as lab-leak evidence, because the zoonotic side exhibits a comparably surprising 'pointed-to-in-advance' coincidence — Eddie Holmes's 2014 photos of the exact HSM shop (6:29) that later had the most positive samples — which tempers the DEFUSE narrative rather than establishing that DEFUSE carries no net asymmetric weight for lab leak.

nodes.label: A-31 - The animal-negative result is uninformative because sampling began after animals were removed
nodes.title: The 0/457 animal-negative result carries almost no evidential weight against animal infection, because animal sampling began on 18 January 2020, weeks after the 1 January market closure and removal of the live susceptible wildlife, so the population that could have been infected was no longer available to sample.

nodes.label: A-32 - Spatial clustering of virus in the wildlife wing co-located with wildlife material fits wildlife-market emergence
nodes.title: The concentration of viral positives in the west/wildlife wing, together with both lineages and abundant susceptible-wildlife genetic material in that same area, is the environmental signature predicted if infected wildlife at those stalls seeded the outbreak, raising the probability of wildlife-associated market emergence.

nodes.label: A-33 - Environmental swabs cannot distinguish whether humans or animals deposited the virus
nodes.title: Because environmental swabs record only where viral RNA was deposited and not which species deposited it, the market surveillance data cannot by themselves distinguish an animal-origin spillover from human introduction into an amplifying venue, leaving the origin-versus-amplifier question open.

nodes.label: A-34 - Widespread market surface contamination is compatible with human shedding and does not by itself demonstrate an infected-animal source
nodes.title: The 73 environmental positives establish widespread surface contamination but not its source: contamination is equally compatible with shedding by infected people, by infected animals, or from contaminated products, and with all 457 animal samples negative, human shedding alone suffices to explain it.

nodes.label: A-35 - Correcting Pekar 2022 asymmetric conditionalization reverses its Bayes factor to at least 4.4 favouring a single introduction
nodes.title: Requiring the two-introduction model I2 to satisfy the same size-ratio and sequence-difference conditions that Pekar et al. 2022 imposed on the one-introduction model I1 collapses P(tau|I2) by roughly an order of magnitude, turning the reported ~4.3 Bayes factor favouring two introductions into a factor of order 4 (approximately 3.9-4.4) favouring a single introduction.

nodes.label: A-36 - Logit-additive combination with dependence handling and uncertainty discounting yields combined odds favouring lab leak
nodes.title: Combining the individual likelihood-ratio factors additively in log-odds (logits), while avoiding double-counting dependent factors and discounting each factor by its uncertainty, yields net odds favouring a research-related origin.

nodes.label: A-37 - Historical lab-leak base rates plus WIV-specific factors set the 1-in-100 to 1-in-200 prior
nodes.title: Pre-COVID lab-accident base rates combined with WIV-specific risk factors and the roughly 50% chance DEFUSE-type work was active justify a prior of about 1/100 to 1/200 for a research-related 2019 origin.

nodes.label: A-38 - The outbreak occurring specifically in Wuhan is a strong likelihood factor for lab origin
nodes.title: That the pandemic began in Wuhan - home to the main labs studying SARS-related bat coronaviruses - is more expected under a lab origin than under zoonosis and is thus a positive likelihood factor favouring lab origin; the size of that factor (Weissman's ~4.4 logits) is contingent on treating zoonotic-spillover probability as tracking raw wildlife-trade volume rather than human population, urbanisation, or detectability.

nodes.label: A-39 - The sarbecovirus lineage, furin cleavage site, and DEFUSE-matching features each favour lab origin
nodes.title: The choice of a sarbecovirus, the presence of an unusual furin cleavage site, and sequence features matching the 2018 DEFUSE proposal each independently raise the likelihood of a research-related origin over zoonosis.

nodes.label: A-4 - Low-coverage lab-clustered intermediates indicate contamination, not real transitional genotypes
nodes.title: Because genomes with about 10x or lower coverage at the diagnostic sites and lineage-specific shared rare mutations are the recognized signature of cross-contamination or bioinformatic error rather than true biological variants, the 20 apparent A/B intermediate genomes provide no evidence of a genuine within-human A-to-B transition.

nodes.label: A-40 - Ascertainment bias, not a market spillover, best explains the Huanan-market case clustering
nodes.title: The early-case clustering at the Huanan market is better explained by biased case-finding than by a true market spillover, and multiple independent strands actively point away from the market.

nodes.label: A-41 - With overdispersed infectiousness, chance superspreader placement, not average R0 or spillover location, governs whether and where a pathogen first cluster appears
nodes.title: With overdispersed individual infectiousness, for a fixed R0 greater variation (lower k) both sharply raises the probability that a single introduction goes stochastically extinct and makes the outbreaks that do establish rarer but more explosive, so whether and in which setting a pathogen's first large visible cluster appears is governed by chance placement of a rare superspreader in the early generations rather than by average transmissibility or by where spillover occurred.

nodes.label: A-42 - The hospital-centred Ebola case series reflects iatrogenic amplification, so the detected cluster locates the amplifying venue not the zoonotic spillover
nodes.title: Because the visible Yambuku case series was produced by needle-borne amplification of a single introduction at a high-throughput medical venue, the hospital's apparent centrality is an artifact of where transmission was amplified and recorded, not where spillover occurred, and the index case's reservoir exposure is left unresolved by the very data that make the hospital look causally central.

nodes.label: A-43 - Both lineages independently centering on the market points to the market as the common emergence locus
nodes.title: That two independently-introduced viral lineages both have their earliest cases centered on the Huanan market is unlikely unless the market was the shared geographic point of emergence, which disfavors an origin elsewhere (including a lab whose expected early-case centroid would sit near the lab, not the market).

nodes.label: A-44 - Market-unlinked cases clustering on the market argues the signal is not mere market-link ascertainment
nodes.title: Because the December cases with no reported market link lived even closer to the market than the market-linked cases, the spatial clustering cannot be fully explained by investigators preferentially finding market-linked cases, supporting a genuine market-centered emergence signal.

nodes.label: A-45 - Positive samples localizing to a live-mammal stall ties earliest contamination to the wildlife trade
nodes.title: The concentration of positive environmental samples at a single live-mammal vendor stall physically ties the earliest detectable market contamination to the wildlife-selling location, raising the probability that emergence occurred through the live-wildlife trade at the market.

nodes.label: A-46 - DEFUSE shows the exact manipulation debated in SARS-CoV-2's genome was proposed -2 years before emergence at the implicated institutions
nodes.title: That the specific manipulation at issue in SARS-CoV-2's genome — inserting a human-specific/furin cleavage site into a bat-SARSr-CoV backbone and testing it in humanized mice — was explicitly proposed by the WIV/EcoHealth/UNC consortium in March 2018, under two years before SARS-CoV-2 emerged in the WIV's own city, sharply raises the prior probability of an engineered lab origin by establishing concrete motive, means, and opportunity.

nodes.label: A-47 - DEFUSE is evidence of intent and capability, not of execution- a rejected proposal, chimera work slated for UNC, using known low-risk SARS-1-related backbones
nodes.title: DEFUSE evidences intent, means and opportunity but not execution: it is a proposal (never carried out as written; DARPA rejected it), it assigned the chimera/reverse-engineering work largely to UNC rather than the WIV, and it specified known, deliberately low-pandemic-risk backbones (WIV1/SHC014, related to SARS-1) rather than anything in SARS-CoV-2's lineage — so it raises but cannot confirm the engineered-origin hypothesis.

nodes.label: A-48 - Concentration of viral positivity at the exact stall selling susceptible mammals raises the probability of an infected-animal source but does not prove infection
nodes.title: That SARS-CoV-2 positivity is spatially concentrated at the one stall where susceptible mammals (raccoon dogs etc.) were sold, with wildlife mtDNA in every positive stall-A sample and humans not the dominant mammal there, is what one expects if an infected animal shed virus at that stall — raising the probability of a wildlife source, though co-location alone cannot prove animal infection.

nodes.label: A-49 - The reported negative correlation between wildlife mtDNA and viral RNA is largely a sampling-design artifact, not evidence against a wildlife source
nodes.title: Univariate correlations finding wildlife mtDNA negatively associated with SARS-CoV-2 across all sequenced samples are biased by the sampling design and by expected animal-before-human shedding plus RNA decay, so a weak or negative correlation is not evidence against an infected-animal source.

nodes.label: A-5 - Both lineages spread without within-human adaptation, implying a pre-adapted animal reservoir
nodes.title: Both lineage A and lineage B established sustained human transmission without requiring adaptive mutations, implying the progenitor was already efficiently human-transmissible in its animal reservoir and making multiple independent zoonotic spillovers from that reservoir plausible.

nodes.label: A-50 - Recovery of narrow-host-range wildlife virus genomes co-located with their hosts implies live, productively infected animals were present in the market
nodes.title: Reconstructing near-complete genomes of viruses with narrow, known host ranges (raccoon-dog amdoparvovirus, bamboo-rat betacoronavirus, civet kobuvirus), each found where its host's mtDNA was and nested phylogenetically among that single host's viruses, implies infected animals of those species (whether live or as freshly-slaughtered carcasses/tissue) were physically present in the market and traded from southern-China farms — ruling out generic contamination or trace residual DNA, but not on its own distinguishing live shedding animals from slaughtered-but-infected ones.

nodes.label: A-51 - Market-associated genomes' tMRCA equalling the global tMRCA, with both lineages present, indicates emergence at the market rather than downstream amplification
nodes.title: If the market outbreak were merely a downstream amplification of pre-existing community transmission, market-associated genomes would coalesce well after the pandemic's root and likely show only the amplified lineage; instead their tMRCA equals the global tMRCA and both lineages A and B are present at the market, indicating the market sits at (not downstream of) the origin.

nodes.label: A-52 - A species absent from the traded stock cannot be the market spillover vector
nodes.title: A species that was never physically present at the markets cannot be the vehicle by which a virus reached humans there; the absence of pangolins and bats from the 47,381 recorded traded animals over 2.5 years therefore makes them very unlikely as the Huanan-market intermediate host or vector, but does not strictly rule them out, because the census captured only observed/recorded trade and could miss unrecorded (e.g. illegally trafficked) presence.

nodes.label: A-53 - Susceptible mammals sold live under high-contact conditions supply the animal-side precondition for market spillover
nodes.title: The sale in volume of known SARS-CoV-2-susceptible mammals (raccoon dog, civet, mink) - the same taxa implicated in the 2003 SARS-CoV-1 market spillover - alive in stacked cages with on-site butchering supplies exactly the high-contact animal-side precondition a market wildlife-to-human spillover requires, raising the plausibility of that route.

nodes.label: A-54 - SARS-CoV-2 tracking fish and livestock rather than raccoon dogs is the pattern expected from human deposition, undercutting the specific raccoon-dog-shedding claim
nodes.title: If raccoon dogs (or bamboo rats) at the market had been shedding SARS-CoV-2, viral RNA abundance should co-vary positively with those species' mtDNA; instead the virus is most abundant where fish, livestock and human material dominate and is negatively associated with raccoon dog, the pattern expected if the RNA was deposited by infected humans onto commonly-handled surfaces — so the metagenomic co-location does not support raccoon dogs as the shedding host.

nodes.label: A-55 - Because the samples were collected a month into widespread human transmission, their viral-animal co-location is uninformative about the outbreak source in either direction
nodes.title: The environmental samples were collected around a month after human-to-human SARS-CoV-2 transmission was already widespread in Wuhan, so co-mingling of viral RNA and animal mtDNA cannot reliably identify an infected intermediate host — and, symmetrically, the lack of viral-animal association does not disprove that infected animals were present; late sampling makes this metagenomic co-location a weak, non-decisive instrument about the source in both directions (rather than strictly zero information).

nodes.label: A-56 - Market-positive-farm-negative split with a within-owner control locates infection at the market
nodes.title: The 100%-positive market vs 0/1,107 farm contrast, and especially the same owner's civets being negative on the farm yet positive at the market, force the inference that infection occurred at the market rather than on the farms or being intrinsic to the animals.

nodes.label: A-57 - Prototype-to-epidemic SNV progression indicates active viral adaptation in civets at the market
nodes.title: A graded SNV series whose zero-mutation prototype is found in market animals and which progresses toward the human epidemic genotype indicates the virus was actively evolving within civets at the market, consistent with market amplification/adaptation rather than a long-established stable civet reservoir.

nodes.label: A-58 - Suboptimal, non-designed-looking RBD argues against rational engineering
nodes.title: Because computational prediction deems the RBD a non-ideal binding solution unlike the residues known to be optimal in SARS-CoV, a rational designer using structure-based methods would not have produced it — which is evidence against structure-based rational design of the RBD specifically. It is not evidence against purposeful manipulation in general, since directed evolution / serial passage would likewise yield a high-affinity yet non-textbook-optimal solution indistinguishable from natural selection.

nodes.label: A-59 - Pangolin coronaviruses proving the optimized RBD exists in nature argues against engineering
nodes.title: Malayan pangolin coronaviruses carry the same six key RBD residues and thus an equally human-ACE2-optimized RBD, showing this solution exists and arises in nature; the SARS-CoV-2 RBD therefore need not have been engineered and is consistent with natural acquisition.

nodes.label: A-6 - Rootclaim base-rate prior- accidental lab escape is a priori very unlikely (~0.6%) versus 78% zoonotic
nodes.title: From historical incident frequencies, Rootclaim's prior odds across origins are Zoonotic 78% : Zoonotic-collection 6% : Bioweapon 16% : Lab-escape 0.6% — before any SARS-CoV-2-specific evidence, an accidental lab escape is a priori very unlikely.

nodes.label: A-60 - Backbone matching no known reverse-genetics system argues against lab construction
nodes.title: Because deliberate engineering (given early-2020 knowledge) would have started from a documented published virus backbone, and SARS-CoV-2's backbone matches none, construction from a known reverse-genetics system is not supported by the genetic data.

nodes.label: A-61 - Natural routes for the furin site plus O-linked glycans argue against a cell-culture origin
nodes.title: The polybasic furin cleavage site, though novel among lineage B sarbecoviruses, has precedent for arising naturally in coronaviruses (recombination/insertion, in-host evolution), and the predicted O-linked glycans flanking it imply the involvement of a functioning immune system in a live host, together arguing against generation by passage in cell culture.

nodes.label: A-62 - Anomalously short longest fragment places SARS-CoV-2's map in the engineered tail of the wild-type distribution
nodes.title: Given the in-vitro-genome-assembly constraints (5-8 fragments, longest <8 kb, even spacing), SARS-CoV-2's BsaI/BsmBI longest fragment (25% of genome) falls in the bottom 1% of a wild-type distribution of natural coronavirus digests and yields a more extreme z-score than any non-engineered virus examined (implying under 0.07% chance naturally), matching published synthetic clones.

nodes.label: A-63 - Exclusively silent mutations concentrated at the restriction sites match the engineering signature
nodes.title: That the BsaI/BsmBI sites differ from RaTG13 (12/12) and BANAL-52 (5/5) by exclusively synonymous mutations at a significantly elevated rate, with only 1.2% (RaTG13) / 0.1% (BANAL-52) of random in-silico mutants reaching an equal-or-greater z-score, matches the requirement that IVGA site modifications be silent and is argued to be unlikely under chance evolution.

nodes.label: A-64 - BsaI sites flanking S1-RBD match a purposeful design for RBD-FCS manipulation
nodes.title: The two BsaI sites flank the S1 region and S1/S2 junction as in prior published chimera work (e.g. WIV1 in Hu et al. 2017), an arrangement that would enable efficient RBD substitution and furin-site insertion - so the map is consistent with a deliberate reverse-genetics design (weak evidence for it), though it does not by itself favour design over an incidental natural map.

nodes.label: A-65 - A wild bat RBD binding hACE2 as well as SARS-CoV-2 shows no lab adaptation is needed for human-ACE2 use
nodes.title: Because an unmodified wild bat virus (BANAL-236) binds human ACE2 with affinity equal to or greater than early SARS-CoV-2 and enters/replicates in human cells, high-affinity human-ACE2 tropism does not require passage or engineering, undercutting the premise that SARS-CoV-2's RBD needed lab adaptation to use hACE2.

nodes.label: A-66 - Mosaic recombination placing SARS-CoV-2's RBD in the wild bat clade supports a natural recombinant origin
nodes.title: The GARD-detected mosaic structure - with SARS-CoV-2's RBD-region fragment nesting within the wild BANAL-52/103/236 clade and more than five identified bat donors - shows SARS-CoV-2's components (apart from the FCS) are present and recombining in wild bats, supporting a natural recombinant origin of the backbone-plus-RBD over a constructed chimera.

nodes.label: A-67 - The furin cleavage site is the one feature not bridged by the closest wild relatives
nodes.title: Since none of the closest wild relatives carries a furin cleavage site while sharing nearly everything else, the FCS is a discriminating feature whose acquisition is a separate event not explained by these bat genomes, leaving its route to be determined.

nodes.label: A-68 - A single natural recombination producing the backbone+RBD+FCS combination is a priori improbable
nodes.title: Producing SARS-CoV-2's RaTG13-like backbone with a pangolin-like RBD by natural recombination would require a RaTG13-like and a pangolin-like virus co-infecting one cell in one host simultaneously - improbable given low pangolin population density, the scarcity of these CoVs in pangolins, and that reconstituted RaTG13 does not bind pangolin ACE2 - so an engineered chimera is a competitive explanation.

nodes.label: A-69 - The out-of-frame furin-site insertion rules out slippage-type natural insertion mechanisms
nodes.title: Because the PRRA insertion is out of frame relative to the aligned RaTG13/MP789 sequences, and insertions produced by polymerase slippage or release/repriming are expected to preserve the reading frame, those natural insertion mechanisms are excluded - leaving recombination or deliberate insertion.

nodes.label: A-7 - Rootclaim- COVID-19's moderate contagion-lethality makes it a poor traditional bioweapon (bioweapon divided by 10)
nodes.title: COVID-19 is more contagious than typical flu but less lethal than MERS/SARS and emerged in peacetime, making it a poor traditional bioweapon — Rootclaim divides the bioweapon hypothesis by ~10.

nodes.label: A-70 - Rare CGG-CGG codon usage plus a conveniently placed FauI site is more expected from lab insertion
nodes.title: The FCS's two arginines use CGG-CGG - a codon pair rare in these viruses and the only doubled CGG in the genome - and the insert conveniently carries a FauI restriction site usable for cloning/screening (RFLP), a pattern the authors argue is more readily explained by human codon choice and restriction-site design than by natural evolution.

nodes.label: A-71 - Chimeric-CoV construction and furin-site insertion were routine, trace-free 2015-2018 techniques
nodes.title: Combining bat backbones with heterologous RBDs, inserting furin cleavage sites, and serial-passage adaptation in hACE2 animals were all established, published pre-2019 techniques (WIV/Baric gain-of-function work, the WIV1 eight-chimera panel, published FCS insertion, no-see-um seamless cloning) that leave no trace, so constructing SARS-CoV-2's genome was technically feasible before the outbreak.

nodes.label: A-72 - Rebuttal- the RBD's human-ACE2 optimization does not preclude rational design
nodes.title: Andersen's claim that a designer could not have predicted SARS-CoV-2's high-affinity human-ACE2 RBD is weakened because structural analysis shows it recognises hACE2 more efficiently than SARS-CoV, chimera work shows bat spikes bind hACE2 with more plasticity than predicted, and WIV1/Rs3367 (which binds hACE2) already shares four of six critical RBD residues - so effective hACE2 binding was foreseeable/selectable in the lab.

nodes.label: A-73 - The two lab-leaning agencies (FBI, DOE) are the ones with the most relevant scientific capability, giving their lean more weight than a bare 2-of-8 headcount, but low-moderate confidence and classified reasoning cap the update
nodes.title: The FBI and DOE are the two tasked agencies with the most directly relevant scientific capability (the FBI's Fort Detrick bioforensics lab; the DOE's national laboratories), so their lab-leaning conclusions carry more evidential weight than a bare 2-of-8 agency headcount suggests — but the update is bounded because both are only low/moderate confidence and their underlying reasoning is classified and unassessable.

nodes.label: A-74 - The FBI and DOE lab positions share a classified evidence pool and are politically salient, so they are not two independent lines of evidence; DOE's reportedly distinct reasoning only partly mitigates this
nodes.title: Because US intelligence assessments draw on a common pool of classified human/signals intelligence and are politically salient, the FBI's and DOE's lab-leaning positions are not fully independent corroboration (shared-sourcing and agency-groupthink risk); WSJ's report that the DOE reached its conclusion for different reasons than the FBI only partially offsets this, so the two positions should count as materially less than two independent lines of evidence for a lab origin.

nodes.label: A-75 - The split, low-confidence IC verdict (2 lab, 4 natural, rest unresolved) shows the IC has not resolved origin, and DOE's lean rests partly on absence-of-evidence, making the institutional record a weak signal either way
nodes.title: The IC verdict is a low-confidence split — 2 agencies lab (moderate/low), 4 natural (low), the rest unresolved — which shows the community as a whole has not resolved the origin; and since the DOE's lab lean rests partly on the continued failure to find a host animal ('absence of evidence') rather than positive lab evidence, the institutional record is only a weak signal, and a weak one in both directions.

nodes.label: A-8 - Rootclaim- outbreak first appearing in Wuhan (home of the WIV) strongly favors a lab origin
nodes.title: That the outbreak first appeared in Wuhan — home to the WIV, one of only a few gain-of-function coronavirus labs — strongly favors a lab origin: zoonotic divided by 20, bioweapon by 15, zoonotic-collection by 2, lab-escape unchanged.

nodes.label: A-9 - Rootclaim- the Wuhan wet-market case cluster favors zoonosis (net factor toward natural spillover)
nodes.title: No obvious natural reservoir exists near Wuhan, yet the initial case cluster at the Wuhan wet market is much more likely under zoonosis; Rootclaim's net treatment favors natural spillover (zoonotic unchanged; lab, bioweapon, zoonotic-collection each divided by 2).

nodes.label: H-14 - The market environmental pattern reflects wildlife-associated emergence at the Huanan market
nodes.title: The Huanan Seafood Market was the primary site at which SARS-CoV-2 first emerged into humans — the geographic epicenter and origin of the outbreak, via the live-wildlife trade — rather than a downstream venue amplifying virus already circulating in the Wuhan community; supported jointly by the environmental viral positives concentrated in the wildlife wing and co-located with susceptible-wildlife genetic material, the presence of both early lineages A and B at the market, the market-associated genomes' tMRCA matching the pandemic tMRCA, and the early-case geography centering on the market.

nodes.label: H-15 - The market may have been an amplification venue seeded by infected humans, not necessarily the origin
nodes.title: The environmental contamination is equally consistent with the Huanan market being a secondary amplification venue into which infected humans (rather than infected animals) introduced an already-circulating virus, so the surveillance data do not establish the market as the origin of the spillover.

nodes.label: H-16 - SARS-CoV-2 early lineages A and B are better explained by a single introduction than by two
nodes.title: Once Pekar et al. 2022's Bayesian calculation is corrected for its conditionalization error, its own model and data favour a single successful introduction of SARS-CoV-2 into humans over two independent introductions, i.e. early lineages A and B most plausibly share one spillover / human MRCA, consistent with Lv et al. 2024's reading of more complete data.

nodes.label: H-19 - The Huanan-market early-case clustering is largely an ascertainment-bias artifact, not evidence of a market spillover
nodes.title: Weissman argues the concentration of early recognized cases around the Huanan Seafood Market reflects biased case-finding (testing concentrated on market-linked people) rather than the true origin, so the market clustering gives little support to a market spillover; several independent strands (phylogenetic ordering, animal-DNA non-correlation, Weibo geolocation near WIV/CDC, Levin's spatial reanalysis) point away from the market.

nodes.label: H-41 - Natural zoonotic spillover with no research involvement
nodes.title: SARS-CoV-2 first infected humans through natural zoonotic spillover: a naturally-evolved sarbecovirus crossed from an animal reservoir or an intermediate host into people through ordinary ecological contact (for example the live-wildlife trade), with no research activity involved in the virus's history or in its entry into humans.

nodes.label: H-42 - Research-related leak of an unmodified naturally-evolved virus (zoonotic collection)
nodes.title: SARS-CoV-2 first infected humans through a research-related pathway involving an unmodified, naturally-evolved virus: a wild virus was collected, sampled, or handled by researchers and reached humans through that activity (for example an infection acquired during field or laboratory work, or a leak of a natural isolate), without any laboratory modification of the virus itself.

nodes.label: H-43 - Research-related origin of a laboratory-manipulated virus (engineered lab leak)
nodes.title: SARS-CoV-2 first infected humans through a research-related incident involving a laboratory-manipulated virus: the virus was shaped by human intervention in a laboratory — gain-of-function engineering, synthetic or reverse-genetics genome assembly, chimera construction, and/or serial passage — before entering humans by an accidental leak (or, in the extreme, a deliberate release).

nodes.label: H-44 - The origin is something not listed here
nodes.title: The answer is something not listed here: SARS-CoV-2's origin is none of natural zoonotic spillover, a research-related leak of an unmodified natural virus, or a research-related incident with a laboratory-manipulated virus — for example a history mixing natural and research elements that none of them captures, or a mechanism no analysis in this corpus has proposed.

nodes.label: H-45 - The market's role is something not listed here
nodes.title: The answer is something not listed here: the Huanan market's causal role in the emergence is none of primary wildlife-to-human spillover site, externally-seeded amplification venue, or non-causal detection artifact — for example some other route or role that none of the named members captures.

nodes.label: H-5 - Early A-B diversity reflects two or more separate zoonotic introductions, not one
nodes.title: The two co-circulating early lineages A and B arose from at least two independent zoonotic introductions of SARS-CoV-2 into humans (both progenitor viruses circulating in non-human mammals beforehand), rather than from a single introduction that then diversified within the human population.

nodes.label: H-11 - Sustained human-to-human transmission was occurring among close contacts by mid-December 2019
nodes.title: Human-to-human transmission of the novel coronavirus was already occurring among close contacts from about the middle of December 2019.

nodes.label: H-18 - The prior probability of a research-related pandemic origin in 2019 is on the order of 1 in 100 to 1 in 200
nodes.title: Weissman estimates the pre-evidence prior probability of a research-related SARS-CoV-2 origin in 2019 at roughly 1/100 to 1/200 (a ~0.2-1%/yr major-leak base rate per relevant lab, conditioned on about a 50% chance DEFUSE-type work was underway at WIV), giving starting prior odds near 1/70 favouring zoonosis, i.e. a lab origin is a priori uncommon but far from negligible.

nodes.label: H-29 - The Xinyuan market, not the farms, was where civets were infected and the virus amplified and spilled to humans
nodes.title: Palm civets were not infected on their farms of origin but became infected and amplified the SARS-CoV-like virus at the live-animal market, which then acted as the source of sporadic human SARS cases.

nodes.label: H-30 - Palm civets are an amplifying intermediate host, not the natural reservoir of SARS-CoV
nodes.title: Palm civets act as a highly susceptible amplifying/intermediate host for SARS-CoV at markets rather than being its natural reservoir, which remains to be identified.

nodes.label: H-7 - No substantial cryptic human circulation of SARS-CoV-2 before December 2019
nodes.title: SARS-CoV-2 was not circulating substantially in humans before December 2019; epidemic simulations consistent with the observed genomic diversity imply a median of zero COVID-19 hospitalizations by 1 December 2019 and only a few cumulative infections at the tMRCA.

nodes.label: H-8 - At least one farm worker was infected by mink (SARS-CoV-2 mink-to-human spillback occurred)
nodes.title: At least one NB1 worker (diagnosed 28 April, after the mink outbreak began) acquired SARS-CoV-2 from the mink rather than from the human community, i.e. mink-to-human (reverse-zoonotic) transmission occurred.

nodes.label: H-9 - Farmed mink are a competent host that can sustain SARS-CoV-2 transmission chains
nodes.title: Farmed mink are a competent SARS-CoV-2 host in which the virus, once introduced, sustains onward mink-to-mink transmission and spreads through the population, rather than requiring repeated independent human introductions.

nodes.label: H-1 - Rootclaim- SARS-CoV-2 was an accidental lab escape of a gain-of-function-engineered virus (~89%)
nodes.title: Rootclaim's Bayesian analysis concludes the single most probable origin (~89%) is that SARS-CoV-2 was developed during gain-of-function research (the WIV implicated) and released by accident.

nodes.label: H-10 - The Huanan market was the initial epicenter, with human-to-human spread taking over
nodes.title: The Huanan Seafood Market was the initial focus of the outbreak (early spillover/amplification site), and human-to-human transmission subsequently carried the virus beyond the market, producing the falling market-linkage fraction over time.

nodes.label: H-12 - Judge Eric Stansifer- zoonotic spillover at the Huanan Seafood Market is the more likely origin (high confidence)
nodes.title: Judge Eric Stansifer's written decision concludes with high confidence that SARS-CoV-2 emerged from zoonotic spillover at the Huanan Seafood Market, and correspondingly assigns the (narrowly-defined WIV gain-of-function) lab-leak hypothesis a low posterior: his worked Bayesian table puts P(zoonotic) at about 99.9% and P(lab leak) at about 0.075% (~1-in-1300), driven mainly by the epidemiological proximity of the earliest cases to a plausible animal source rather than a lab source, with the caveat that the precise figure is not to be taken in earnest while the qualitative strong-zoonotic conclusion is robust.

nodes.label: H-13 - Judge Eric Stansifer- a WIV gain-of-function lab leak is the less likely origin (P about 0.075%, ~1-in-1300)
nodes.title: Stansifer's worked Bayesian table assigns the lab-leak hypothesis (SARS-CoV-2 from gain-of-function research at the WIV) a posterior P(LL|evidence) about 0.075% (~1-in-1300), while cautioning the precise number is not to be taken in earnest and that dampening the inputs for uncertainty still cannot push the probability across the 50% line.

nodes.label: H-17 - SARS-CoV-2 most likely originated from a research-related incident rather than natural zoonosis (Weissman synthesis)
nodes.title: Weissman's combined Bayesian analysis concludes a research-related (lab) origin of SARS-CoV-2 is substantially more probable than natural zoonotic spillover: point-estimate combined odds favour lab leak strongly (P(zoonosis) shrinking below about 1%; framings across versions range from roughly 4:1 up to about 165:1), pulled back toward single-digit to low-double-digit odds once realistic uncertainties are integrated.

nodes.label: H-2 - Rootclaim- SARS-CoV-2 was an engineered bioweapon deliberately released (~4.5%)
nodes.title: Rootclaim assigns ~4.5% to SARS-CoV-2 having been genetically engineered as a bioweapon and deliberately released.

nodes.label: H-20 - SARS-CoV-2 was introduced to the Huanan market via cold-chain - frozen imported products
nodes.title: SARS-CoV-2 was introduced into the Huanan market on cold-chain or frozen imported products rather than through a live infected animal or an infected person, with the frozen supply chain (from 20 countries, some with SARS-CoV-2 reported before end-2019) carrying the virus into the market.

nodes.label: H-21 - The Huanan market was the early epicenter of the pandemic via the live-wildlife trade
nodes.title: SARS-CoV-2 emerged into humans via the live-wildlife trade at the Huanan Seafood Market, which was the geographic epicenter of the earliest COVID-19 cases.

nodes.label: H-22 - SARS-CoV-2's furin cleavage site was inserted via DEFUSE-type gain-of-function engineering at the WIV-EcoHealth-UNC consortium
nodes.title: The lab-leak reading of DEFUSE: SARS-CoV-2 — in particular its furin cleavage site inserted into a bat-SARSr-CoV backbone — was the product of the specific gain-of-function manipulation proposed in DEFUSE, carried out (in modified form) at the WIV/EcoHealth/UNC consortium and then leaked.

nodes.label: H-23 - Susceptible wildlife sold at Huanan market wildlife stall A (raccoon dogs and-or related mammals) were the intermediate host of the SARS-CoV-2 spillover
nodes.title: Live susceptible mammals sold at the Huanan market's wildlife stalls — raccoon dogs prominent, alongside civets, bamboo rats and others concentrated at stall A — were infected with SARS-CoV-2 and were the intermediate host through which the virus spilled over to humans at the market.

nodes.label: H-24 - SARS-CoV-2 emerged at the Huanan market rather than circulating in the Wuhan community beforehand
nodes.title: SARS-CoV-2 first emerged and began spreading at/around the Huanan market itself (the market was the origin/epicenter of the outbreak), rather than the market outbreak being a downstream amplification of pre-existing, unrelated community transmission in Wuhan.

nodes.label: H-25 - Pangolins and bats were not the Huanan-market intermediate host or vector
nodes.title: Pangolins (and bats) were not the intermediate host or vector by which SARS-CoV-2 reached humans at the Wuhan markets, since neither was traded there.

nodes.label: H-26 - The Wuhan market wildlife trade was a viable SARS-CoV-2 spillover route
nodes.title: The pre-pandemic Wuhan wildlife trade constituted a viable route for a SARS-CoV-2 wildlife-to-human spillover, because live SARS-CoV-2-susceptible mammals were sold in volume under high-contact conditions at these markets (including Huanan).

nodes.label: H-27 - US intelligence- the FBI and DOE assess a lab-associated Wuhan origin of COVID-19 as most likely
nodes.title: The two US intelligence agencies with the most relevant scientific capability — the FBI (moderate confidence) and, since Feb 2023, the DOE (low confidence) — assess that COVID-19 most likely originated from a laboratory incident in Wuhan.

nodes.label: H-28 - US intelligence- a plurality of tasked agencies assess a natural animal origin of COVID-19 as most likely (low confidence)
nodes.title: A plurality of the tasked US intelligence agencies (four of the eight involved) assess, with low confidence, that COVID-19 most likely arose naturally from an animal — the natural-origin institutional position that stands against the FBI/DOE lab lean.

nodes.label: H-3 - Rootclaim- SARS-CoV-2 was a naturally-evolved virus contracted by researchers (zoonotic collection, ~3.2%)
nodes.title: Rootclaim assigns ~3.2% to a zoonotic-collection origin: the virus evolved in nature and was then contracted by/leaked via virus researchers, without genetic engineering.

nodes.label: H-31 - SARS-CoV-2 arose by natural evolution, not laboratory engineering
nodes.title: SARS-CoV-2 is not a product of purposeful genetic manipulation or a laboratory construct; its distinctive features are the product of natural evolutionary processes.

nodes.label: H-32 - SARS-CoV-2's human-adapted features arose by natural selection in an animal host before zoonotic transfer
nodes.title: The optimized RBD and the polybasic furin cleavage site were acquired through natural selection in an animal reservoir (e.g. bats/pangolins) prior to the virus jumping to humans.

nodes.label: H-33 - Some SARS-CoV-2 features arose by natural selection during cryptic human-to-human transmission after an early spillover
nodes.title: A progenitor jumped to humans carrying an already-optimized RBD and subsequently acquired the polybasic furin cleavage site (and other adaptations) during undetected human-to-human transmission.

nodes.label: H-34 - SARS-CoV-2 originated as an infectious clone assembled in vitro (a reverse-genetics system)
nodes.title: SARS-CoV-2's genome was assembled in vitro from lab-synthesized fragments via a Type IIS restriction-enzyme reverse-genetics / in-vitro genome-assembly system — with the BsaI/BsmBI sites (notably the pair flanking S1/RBD) reflecting purposeful placement to enable receptor-binding-domain swaps and furin-site insertion — i.e. it has a synthetic, deliberately-engineered origin.

nodes.label: H-35 - SARS-CoV-2's restriction map was designed to enable RBD swaps and furin-site insertion at S1-S2
nodes.title: The two BsaI sites flanking the S1 region and RBD were purposefully placed to permit efficient substitution of the receptor-binding domain and insertion of a furin cleavage site at the S1/S2 junction, matching a deliberate chimera-construction design.

nodes.label: H-36 - Wild bat sarbecoviruses able to infect humans via hACE2 without adaptation circulate in the Indochinese peninsula
nodes.title: SARS-CoV-2-like bat sarbecoviruses that bind human ACE2 and enter/replicate in human cells with no laboratory adaptation circulate naturally in Rhinolophus bats of the Indochinese peninsula (northern Laos).

nodes.label: H-37 - SARS-CoV-2's human-ACE2-binding RBD and backbone arose by natural recombination among wild bat viruses
nodes.title: SARS-CoV-2's genome apart from the furin cleavage site - including its human-ACE2-optimized RBD - is explained by natural recombination among wild bat sarbecoviruses of this Indochinese pool, requiring no laboratory engineering or adaptation of the RBD.

nodes.label: H-38 - SARS-CoV-2's furin cleavage site was acquired separately, by a route not represented in the closest bat relatives
nodes.title: Because the closest wild relatives all lack a furin cleavage site, SARS-CoV-2 acquired its FCS by a distinct later event whose route (natural or otherwise) is not accounted for by these bat genomes.

nodes.label: H-39 - SARS-CoV-2 is a laboratory-engineered chimera (RaTG13-like backbone + pangolin-like RBD + site-directed furin site)
nodes.title: SARS-CoV-2 was created in the lab by combining a RaTG13-like bat backbone with a pangolin-CoV-like RBD and inserting the furin cleavage site by site-directed mutagenesis, producing a chimera that would appear natural.

nodes.label: H-4 - Rootclaim- SARS-CoV-2 arose by natural zoonotic spillover (~3.2%)
nodes.title: Rootclaim assigns ~3.2% to natural zoonotic spillover — the virus evolved in nature and was transmitted to humans zoonotically (no lab involvement).

nodes.label: H-40 - SARS-CoV-2's RBD-hACE2 fit was perfected by serial passage in cell culture or humanized-ACE2 animals
nodes.title: The optimal adaptation of SARS-CoV-2's RBD to human ACE2 (and possibly the O-linked glycans) could have been achieved by serial passage of a chimeric or bat virus in cell culture or in humanized/hACE2-expressing animals in the lab.

nodes.label: H-6 - The A-B intermediate haplotypes did not genuinely circulate in humans
nodes.title: No genuine intermediate genotype (C/C or T/T) connecting lineages A and B circulated in humans; the apparent intermediate genomes are contamination or bioinformatic artifacts.
