nodes.label: Main question
nodes.title: Did SARS-CoV-2 first infect humans via zoonotic spillover or a lab leak?

nodes.label: O-1 - Recomputed Monte Carlo p-values for the market as case center are marginal and weaken toward non-significance as the case sample shrinks
nodes.title: Re-running Worobey et al.'s population-density Monte Carlo test, Stoyan & Chiu find the Huanan market is only marginally and unstably identified as the geometric center of the 155 early Wuhan cases; p-values fall from centroid 0.030 / center-point 0.008 / mode 0.070 at n=155 to 0.122 / 0.068 / 0.200 at n=80.

nodes.label: O-17 - None of 457 animal-source samples from the Huanan market tested positive for SARS-CoV-2
nodes.title: Of 457 animal-source samples (18 species, collected from 18 January 2020 as unsold refrigerator/freezer contents, stray-animal swabs and fish-tank contents), none tested positive for SARS-CoV-2.

nodes.label: O-18 - SARS-CoV-2-positive environmental samples were concentrated in the market's western (wildlife) wing
nodes.title: The SARS-CoV-2-positive environmental samples were spatially concentrated in the western wing of the Huanan market (about 87.5% of positives), the area where wildlife vendors were located, with positivity increasing near and within a wildlife stall.

nodes.label: O-19 - Both early lineages A and B recovered from environmental swabs in the market's west wing
nodes.title: Four near-complete SARS-CoV-2 genomes were recovered from Huanan market environmental swabs - A20 (lineage A, 8782T/28144C) and B5, F13, F54 (lineage B) - all from the western/wildlife wing, showing both early lineages A and B were present at the market.

nodes.label: O-2 - Bootstrap places the market at the edge of the case-centroid cloud with Wanda Plaza an equally admissible center
nodes.title: In Stoyan & Chiu's bootstrap of the early-case centroid, the Huanan market lies at the edge of the cloud of resampled centroids, while the nearby Wanda Plaza landmark falls inside the confidence regions of all three center definitions at n=80 — i.e. it is a statistically equally admissible spatial center of the early cases.

nodes.label: O-20 - Positive environmental samples contained abundant genetic material from wildlife species
nodes.title: RNA-sequencing of SARS-CoV-2-positive versus -negative environmental samples showed an abundance of genetic material from many vertebrate genera at the market, including susceptible mammal species (e.g. raccoon dog, civet) present in the same wildlife-stall area as the viral positives.

nodes.label: O-21 - Pekar 2022 reported two-introduction Bayes factor fell from ~60 to ~4.3 after the 2024 coding-error erratum
nodes.title: In Pekar et al. 2022 the originally reported Bayes factor favouring a two-successful-introduction model over a single-introduction model was about 60; after three coding errors flagged post-publication by McCowan were corrected in a 2024 online erratum, the reported Bayes factor fell to about 4.3 in the current version of record, which remains its headline support for separate introductions of lineages A and B.

nodes.label: O-22 - Pekar 2022 computed the two-introduction likelihood by squaring only the polytomy criterion, omitting the size-ratio and sequence-difference conditions imposed on one introduction
nodes.title: Pekar et al. 2022 evaluated the single-introduction likelihood as P(tau|I1)=0.031 by requiring simulations to meet four topology conditions (basal polytomy, lineage sizes between 30 and 70% i.e. |ln SR|<=ln(7/3), sequence difference D=2 with no intermediate genomes, and MRCA placement), but did not simulate the two-introduction model, instead setting P(tau|I2)=P(tau=tauP|I1)^2=0.475^2=0.226 from the polytomy criterion alone (times a ~0.6 MRCA factor), omitting the size-ratio and sequence-difference conditions.

nodes.label: O-23 - Individual infectiousness is highly overdispersed across eight directly-transmitted diseases; SARS dispersion k approx 0.16
nodes.title: Fitting Poisson, geometric and negative-binomial offspring distributions to contact-tracing data from eight directly-transmitted human diseases, Lloyd-Smith et al. find individual infectiousness (individual reproductive number nu, gamma-distributed with mean R0 and dispersion k) is highly right-skewed and the homogeneous-Poisson model is almost always strongly rejected: for SARS (Singapore 2003) k=0.16 (90% CI 0.11-0.64), with 73% of cases having nu<1 and 6% having nu>8; measles shows similarly high variation, smallpox and monkeypox intermediate, pneumonic plague slightly less.

nodes.label: O-25 - Almost all Yambuku Ebola cases traced to reused hospital needles or contact with a case, and the outbreak collapsed when the hospital closed
nodes.title: Transmission was dominated by iatrogenic amplification at Yambuku Mission Hospital: almost all cases after the index had either received injections at the hospital (via reused, unsterilized needles and syringes) or had had close contact with another case; most cases occurred in the first four weeks, after which the hospital closed with 11 of its 17 staff dead of the disease.

nodes.label: O-26 - The natural reservoir and the index case exposure route were not identified by the 1976 Ebola investigation
nodes.title: The 1976 investigation did not identify the animal reservoir or the index case's original source of infection: a search between Bumba Zone and the Sudan (Nzara/Maridi) outbreak failed to find a definite link, and the disease was hitherto unknown in the region, leaving the antecedent zoonotic spillover unresolved by the outbreak record.

nodes.label: O-27 - Nationwide survey of -80,000 wildlife, livestock and poultry samples across 31 Chinese provinces found zero SARS-CoV-2 positives
nodes.title: A nationwide Chinese survey of >80,000 animal samples (38,515 livestock/poultry + 41,696 wild-animal samples) from 31 provinces, collected 2018-2020 before and after the outbreak, found no SARS-CoV-2 antibody or nucleic-acid positive; no relative of SARS-CoV-2 was detected in bats or wildlife across China either.

nodes.label: O-28 - All 457 animal-related market samples (188 individuals, 18 species) were SARS-CoV-2 negative, but were leftover frozen goods and vermin sampled after market closure
nodes.title: 457 animal-related samples from 188 individuals across 18 species in and around the Huanan market (collected 1 Jan-2 Mar 2020) were all SARS-CoV-2 NAT-negative; but the sampled items were unsold frozen/refrigerated goods, warehouse stock, and vector animals (stray cats/dogs, rats) collected after the market was closed and live animals removed — no live traded mammals were available to test.

nodes.label: O-29 - 923 Huanan-market environmental samples yielded 73 SARS-CoV-2 positives (3 isolates), with positives clustering at aquatic-cold-chain-meat vendor stalls
nodes.title: Of 923 environmental samples collected in the Huanan market (1 Jan-2 Mar 2020), 73 were SARS-CoV-2 positive and infectious virus was isolated from 3, revealing widespread surface contamination; 44 of the 73 positives came from stalls of 21 vendors dealing in aquatic animals (13), cold-chain products (16), poultry meat (6), seafood (6), livestock meat (5), vegetables (2) and farmed-wildlife meat (1).

nodes.label: O-30 - December-2019 Wuhan cases were significantly closer to the Huanan market than a population-weighted null
nodes.title: The 155 geolocated December-2019 Wuhan-resident cases had a median residential distance of 4.28 km to the Huanan market, far closer than a population-density-weighted null (median 16.11 km; p<0.001).

nodes.label: O-31 - Both lineage A and lineage B early cases cluster near the Huanan market
nodes.title: Lineage-B December cases (n=11) had a median distance of 8.30 km to the market (p=0.017), and the two earliest-known lineage-A cases were near the market (one at 2.31 km, p=0.034; the other staying at a hotel within ~500 m), so both viral lineages independently cluster near the market.

nodes.label: O-32 - Market-unlinked December cases lived even closer to the market than market-linked cases
nodes.title: December cases with no reported link to the Huanan market (n=120, median 4.00 km) lived significantly closer to the market than cases with a direct market link (n=35, median 5.74 km; p=0.029).

nodes.label: O-33 - Positive market environmental samples concentrated at a single live-mammal vendor stall
nodes.title: Five SARS-CoV-2-positive environmental samples came from a single live-mammal vendor stall in the market's south-west corner (a cage, carts and a hair/feather remover), and spatial relative-risk analysis confirmed positive samples were concentrated where live mammals were sold (p<0.05).

nodes.label: O-35 - DEFUSE was a consortium of EcoHealth, UNC (Baric), the Wuhan Institute of Virology (Shi), Duke-NUS, USGS and PARC, with WIV assigned viral testing and humanized-mouse work
nodes.title: The proposal named EcoHealth Alliance as lead, subcontracting to UNC-Chapel Hill (Ralph Baric; spike reverse-engineering/chimera construction), the Wuhan Institute of Virology (Shi Zhengli group; viral testing, binding assays and some humanized-mouse work), Duke-NUS Singapore (Wang), USGS National Wildlife Health Center (Rocke), and Palo Alto Research Center.

nodes.label: O-36 - DEFUSE proposed inserting field-sampled bat spike proteins into WIV1-SHC014 backbones and infecting humanized (hACE2) mice to assess SARS-like disease capacity
nodes.title: In Thrust Area 1 the proposal planned to sequence and reverse-engineer spike proteins from newly-sampled bat SARSr-CoVs, insert them into bat-SARSr-CoV (WIV1, SHC014) backbones, and use the resulting chimeric viruses to infect humanized (hACE2-transgenic) mice and assess their capacity to cause SARS-like disease.

nodes.label: O-37 - DEFUSE proposed introducing human-specific (furin) cleavage sites into SARS-related coronavirus spikes
nodes.title: The proposal planned to screen SARS-CoV spike sequences for proteolytic and potential furin cleavage sites and, 'where clear mismatches occur,' to introduce 'appropriate human-specific cleavage sites' into the viruses and test their growth in human airway (HAE) and Vero cell cultures.

nodes.label: O-38 - DEFUSE asserted its chimeric-spike work used bat-SARSr-CoV backbones and was 'exempt from dual use and gain of function concerns'
nodes.title: The proposal characterized its plan to insert novel spike proteins into WIV1/SHC014 bat-SARSr-CoV backbones as using bat backbones (not SARS-CoV) and therefore 'exempt from dual use and gain of function concerns' — the document's own risk framing.

nodes.label: O-39 - Market-associated SARS-CoV-2 genomes' tMRCA matches the global pandemic tMRCA and both lineages A and B are present in market environmental samples
nodes.title: The time to most-recent-common-ancestor (tMRCA) of SARS-CoV-2 genomes recovered from Huanan-market environmental samples overlaps the tMRCA of the global pandemic (late Nov-Dec 2019, overlapping 95% HPD intervals), and both early lineages A and B are present in market environmental samples (lineage A conclusively in sample A20).

nodes.label: O-4 - Early SARS-CoV-2 split into two lineages A and B differing by two linked substitutions
nodes.title: Among near-complete SARS-CoV-2 genomes sampled by mid-February 2020, early diversity was dominated by two lineages, A and B, distinguished by exactly two linked nucleotide substitutions at positions 8782 and 28144 (lineage B = C8782/T28144, lineage A = T8782/C28144).

nodes.label: O-40 - SARS-CoV-2 positivity concentrated at wildlife stall A, whose five positive samples all contained susceptible-mammal (incl. raccoon dog) mtDNA
nodes.title: SARS-CoV-2-positive environmental samples were spatially concentrated at one wildlife stall (stall A: 30% qPCR-positive, 3/10; supported by qPCR, metatranscriptomics, and the drain directly in front); all five SARS-CoV-2-positive samples from stall A contained mtDNA of susceptible mammals (raccoon dogs, hoary bamboo rats, others), with humans not the most abundant mammal in those samples, and raccoon dog + hoary bamboo rat the two most commonly detected wildlife species across stalls.

nodes.label: O-41 - In the balanced n=70 wildlife-stall set human mtDNA was uncorrelated with viral RNA while Malayan porcupine and Himalayan marmot mtDNA were significantly positively correlated
nodes.title: In the balanced set of 70 wildlife-stall samples (all sequenced regardless of qPCR status), human mtDNA was not significantly correlated with SARS-CoV-2 RNA (Spearman rho=0.13, 95% CI [-0.09,0.34]; average mammal rho=0.08), whereas Malayan porcupine (rho=0.45, P<0.001) and Himalayan marmot (rho=0.34, P<0.033) mtDNA were significantly positively correlated after FDR (5%) correction.

nodes.label: O-42 - Host-specific wildlife viruses (raccoon-dog amdoparvovirus, bamboo-rat betacoronavirus, civet kobuvirus) were recovered from the market, phylogenetically traceable to southern-China farms
nodes.title: Mostly-complete genomes of three host-specific wildlife viruses — a raccoon-dog amdoparvovirus, a bamboo-rat betacoronavirus, and a civet kobuvirus — were reconstructed from market samples, each phylogenetically nested among viruses shed by a single host species, and co-located with that host's mtDNA; the bamboo-rat betacoronavirus was a close recent relative of a virus from bamboo rats on a Guangxi farm (2019).

nodes.label: O-43 - 47,381 wild animals of 38 species were sold at four Wuhan markets in 2017-2019, including 7 wild-animal shops at Huanan
nodes.title: Systematic monthly surveys (May 2017-November 2019) of 17 live-wild-animal shops across four Wuhan markets documented 47,381 individual animals from 38 terrestrial wild species (31 protected); seven of those shops were at the Huanan Seafood Market.

nodes.label: O-44 - SARS-CoV-2-susceptible mammals (raccoon dogs, civets, mink) were sold live in volume at Wuhan markets
nodes.title: Known SARS-CoV-2-susceptible mammals were on sale in volume, with reported monthly averages of raccoon dog 38.3 +/- 17.2, masked palm civet 10.7 +/- 8.4, American mink 10.4 +/- 1.9, and hog badger 6.8 +/- 5.4 individuals (plus Chinese bamboo rat 42.8 +/- 20.7).

nodes.label: O-47 - In Feb 2023 the DOE shifted to assessing with 'low confidence' that COVID-19 most likely came from a Wuhan lab leak
nodes.title: In a classified intelligence-community update provided to the White House and Congress (reported by the Wall Street Journal on 26 Feb 2023), the U.S. Department of Energy shifted from having no position to assessing, with 'low confidence,' that COVID-19 most likely originated from a laboratory leak in Wuhan.

nodes.label: O-48 - The FBI has assessed with 'moderate confidence' that COVID-19 most likely came from a lab incident in Wuhan
nodes.title: Since the 2021 intelligence-community investigation, the FBI has assessed with 'moderate confidence' that COVID-19 most likely resulted from a laboratory incident in Wuhan — the strongest-confidence lab-leaning position among the US agencies.

nodes.label: O-49 - The 2021 US IC origins investigation was a split verdict- FBI lab (moderate), four agencies natural (low confidence), DOE then agnostic
nodes.title: President Biden's May-2021 IC origins investigation involved 8 of the 18 US intelligence agencies and produced a split verdict: the FBI assessed a lab leak (moderate confidence), four other agencies assessed a probable natural animal origin (low confidence), and the DOE was then agnostic — so after DOE's 2023 shift, 2 of the 8 involved agencies favor a lab origin.

nodes.label: O-5 - The 20 apparent A-B intermediate genomes are low-coverage and lab-clustered
nodes.title: The 20 early genomes carrying an intermediate haplotype (C/C or T/T, i.e. only one of the two lineage-defining mutations) had low sequencing coverage (about 10x or less) at the diagnostic positions and frequently shared rare mutations with, and were sequenced in the same laboratory as, lineage A or B genomes.

nodes.label: O-50 - Across market samples SARS-CoV-2 abundance correlates most with fish and livestock mtDNA (bass, catfish, cow, carp, snakehead) and negatively with raccoon dog and bamboo rat
nodes.title: Across the Huanan-market environmental samples, the chordate species whose mitochondrial reads correlate most strongly with SARS-CoV-2 read abundance are fish and livestock — the five most-correlated are largemouth bass, catfish, cow, carp and snakehead fish — while raccoon dog and hoary bamboo rat mtDNA are negatively correlated with SARS-CoV-2 (correlations computed as Pearson on log-log reads, robust to the Theil-Sen estimator and to date-subsetting, which keeps the raccoon-dog correlation negative).

nodes.label: O-51 - Of 14 samples dominated by raccoon-dog mtDNA only one contained any SARS-CoV-2 read (1 of ~2.1x10^8); all six bamboo-rat-dominated samples had zero
nodes.title: Of the 14 environmental samples whose mitochondrial composition was at least 20% raccoon dog, only one (Q61) contained any SARS-CoV-2 reads at all — a single read out of ~2.1x10^8 high-quality reads — and the other 13 had zero; all six samples dominated by hoary bamboo rat mtDNA likewise had zero SARS-CoV-2 reads.

nodes.label: O-52 - FBI Director Wray stated on-record (28 Feb 2023) that the FBI assesses COVID-19's origin as 'most likely a potential lab incident in Wuhan'
nodes.title: On 28 Feb 2023, FBI Director Christopher Wray said on the record (Fox News) that 'the FBI has for quite some time now assessed that the origins of the pandemic are most likely a potential lab incident in Wuhan,' adding that the Chinese government 'has been doing its best to try to thwart and obfuscate' the investigation — the first public, on-record confirmation of the FBI's assessment.

nodes.label: O-53 - The FBI-DOE lab-leaning assessments are classified and self-rated low-moderate confidence; DOE reportedly reached its view via reasoning distinct from the FBI's
nodes.title: The underlying intelligence is classified and not published; the DOE would not disclose its evidence, the assessment is 'not conclusive' (no identified triggering lab episode linked to the outbreak), and per WSJ the DOE reached its lab-leaning view through different reasoning than the FBI — citing the continued failure over three years to find a host animal plus the nature of Wuhan's coronavirus research.

nodes.label: O-54 - All market palm civets and raccoon dogs at Xinyuan market tested positive for SARS-CoV-like virus
nodes.title: At the Xinyuan live-animal market (Guangdong, January 2004), all 91 palm civets and all 15 raccoon dogs sampled tested RT-PCR positive for a SARS-CoV-like virus, and 22 of 24 environmental swabs (cages, walls, cash tables) were positive.

nodes.label: O-57 - Graded SNV series in the civet spike gene from a zero-mutation prototype to human epidemic strains
nodes.title: Sequencing the spike gene across market civets and raccoon dogs defined 27 signature nucleotide variations (SNVs) forming a graded series: a prototype group with 0 SNVs (two raccoon dogs and one civet), market civets plus an early patient with 2-7 SNVs, and human epidemic strains with 16-27 SNVs.

nodes.label: O-58 - SARS-CoV-2 RBD has six ACE2-contact residues, five differing from SARS-CoV
nodes.title: The SARS-CoV-2 receptor-binding domain carries six residues key to human-ACE2 binding (L455, F486, Q493, S494, N501, Y505); five of the six differ from the corresponding residues in SARS-CoV.

nodes.label: O-59 - Computational analysis predicts the SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 interaction is not ideal - non-optimal
nodes.title: Computational structural analyses predict that the SARS-CoV-2 RBD-human-ACE2 interaction is 'not ideal' and that its RBD sequence differs from the residues shown in SARS-CoV to be optimal for receptor binding, despite the virus binding hACE2 with high affinity in practice.

nodes.label: O-6 - Molecular-clock dating places tMRCA in mid-December 2019 and lineage B primary infection around 18 Nov 2019
nodes.title: BEAST phylodynamic dating of the early genomes estimated the SARS-CoV-2 tMRCA at ~11 December 2019 (95% HPD 25 Nov–12 Dec), the lineage B primary-case infection at ~18 November 2019 (95% HPD 23 October–8 December), and the lineage A primary case at ~25 November 2019 (95% HPD 29 October–14 December).

nodes.label: O-60 - Polybasic furin cleavage site (PRRA insertion) at the S1-S2 junction, absent in related lineage B betacoronaviruses
nodes.title: SARS-CoV-2 has a polybasic (furin) cleavage site formed by a 12-nucleotide insertion adding the amino acids PRRA (yielding an RRAR motif) at the S1/S2 boundary of the spike; such sites are absent in the related lineage B betacoronaviruses.

nodes.label: O-61 - Three predicted O-linked glycosylation sites flank the SARS-CoV-2 furin cleavage site
nodes.title: The proline in the inserted PRRA is predicted to create O-linked glycans at residues S673, T678 and S686 flanking the furin cleavage site.

nodes.label: O-62 - SARS-CoV-2 backbone matches no previously described coronavirus reverse-genetics system
nodes.title: The SARS-CoV-2 genomic backbone is not derived from, and does not match, any previously described/used coronavirus backbone or published reverse-genetics platform.

nodes.label: O-64 - Malayan pangolin coronaviruses share all six key RBD residues with SARS-CoV-2
nodes.title: Coronaviruses from Malayan pangolins show strong RBD similarity to SARS-CoV-2, including identity at all six key ACE2-contact RBD residues.

nodes.label: O-65 - SARS-CoV-2 has five BsaI-BsmBI sites yielding six fragments with unique overhangs
nodes.title: Double digestion of the SARS-CoV-2 genome with the Type IIS enzymes BsaI/BsmBI cuts at 5 recognition sites, producing 6 fragments; all 5 resulting 4-nucleotide overhangs are unique and non-palindromic.

nodes.label: O-66 - SARS-CoV-2's longest BsaI-BsmBI fragment is 7,578 bp (25% of genome) and it lacks two conserved BsaI sites
nodes.title: The longest fragment from BsaI/BsmBI digestion of SARS-CoV-2 is 7,578 bp, 25% of the genome; its restriction map is evenly spaced and lacks two highly conserved BsaI sites found in almost all other lineage B sarbecoviruses.

nodes.label: O-67 - All BsaI-BsmBI-site differences from RaTG13-BANAL-52 are silent, at an elevated rate
nodes.title: All 12 nucleotide differences at BsaI/BsmBI sites between RaTG13 and SARS-CoV-2, and all 5 between BANAL-52 and SARS-CoV-2, are synonymous (silent); the rate of silent mutations within these recognition sites is significantly higher than across the rest of the genome (RaTG13 OR=8.9, P=9e-8; BANAL-52 OR=5.2, P=0.004).

nodes.label: O-68 - Three new Lao bat sarbecoviruses; BANAL-52 is 96.8% identical to SARS-CoV-2, exceeding RaTG13
nodes.title: Three new sarbecoviruses were isolated from cave bats in northern Laos - BANAL-52 (Rhinolophus malayanus), BANAL-103 (R. pusillus) and BANAL-236 (R. marshalli); BANAL-52 is 96.8% identical to SARS-CoV-2 across the whole genome, higher than RaTG13's 96.1%, making it the closest known full-genome relative.

nodes.label: O-69 - BANAL RBDs match 15-16 of 17 hACE2-contact residues vs only 11-17 for RaTG13
nodes.title: Of the 17 RBD residues that contact human ACE2, 16/17 are conserved in BANAL-52/103 (one mismatch, H498Q) and 15/17 in BANAL-236 (K493Q, H498Q), versus only 13/17 for the Cambodian R. shameli virus and 11/17 for RaTG13 - the BANAL RBDs are far closer to SARS-CoV-2 at the receptor interface than RaTG13.

nodes.label: O-7 - 55% of pre-January-2020 cases were Huanan-market-linked versus 8.6% of later cases
nodes.title: Among the first 425 confirmed Wuhan cases, 55% of those with symptom onset before 1 January 2020 were linked to the Huanan Seafood Wholesale Market, versus only 8.6% of cases with onset on or after 1 January 2020.

nodes.label: O-70 - BANAL RBDs bind human ACE2 ~3x more strongly than early SARS-CoV-2; BANAL-236 RBD-hACE2 crystal near-identical
nodes.title: By biolayer interferometry, the BANAL-52/103 and BANAL-236 RBDs bind human ACE2 with a dissociation constant three times lower (higher affinity) than the early Wuhan SARS-CoV-2 RBD; the BANAL-236 RBD-hACE2 crystal structure (PDB 7PKI, 2.9 A) is essentially identical to SARS-CoV-2 (Ca RMSD 0.36 A).

nodes.label: O-71 - A wild, unmodified BANAL-236 enters and replicates in human cells via hACE2
nodes.title: BANAL-236 spike-pseudotyped lentivirus enters hACE2-expressing HEK-293T cells (but not ACE2-negative controls) and is blocked by SARS-CoV-2-neutralizing sera, and live BANAL-236 virus replicates in human Calu-3 and Caco-2 cells expressing endogenous ACE2 - a wild bat virus infecting human cells without adaptation.

nodes.label: O-72 - None of the BANAL viruses has a furin cleavage site
nodes.title: None of the Laotian BANAL sarbecoviruses carries a furin cleavage site at the spike S1/S2 junction; the internal fusion peptide is conserved.

nodes.label: O-73 - SARS-CoV-2 is a recombinant mosaic of -5 wild bat sarbecoviruses, its RBD fragment nesting in the BANAL clade
nodes.title: A GARD recombination analysis found 14 breakpoints (15 fragments); SARS-CoV-2's genome is a mosaic contributed to by more than five bat sarbecoviruses (RmYN02, RpYN06, RaTG13, BANAL-52, BANAL-103), with a breakpoint 7 nt upstream of the RBD and the RBD-containing fragment grouping with the wild BANAL-52/103/236 clade; no pangolin coronavirus was immediately associated with a recombination event at the origin of SARS-CoV-2.

nodes.label: O-74 - SARS-CoV-2 has a RaTG13-like backbone but a pangolin-CoV-like RBD (chimeric structure)
nodes.title: SARS-CoV-2 is ~96.2% identical genome-wide to bat CoV RaTG13 (its closest full-genome relative) and 79% to SARS-CoV, yet its RBD is nearly identical to the pangolin coronavirus MP789: SARS-CoV-2 and pangolin CoV share identical amino acids at the five critical RBD contact residues while RaTG13 shares only one - a chimeric backbone-vs-RBD split.

nodes.label: O-75 - The furin site is a 12-nt PRRA insert with a rare doubled CGG-CGG arginine codon and a FauI site
nodes.title: The SARS-CoV-2 furin cleavage site is created by a 12-nucleotide insert (TCCTCGGCGGGC) coding PRRA at the S1/S2 junction; the two adjacent arginines are encoded by CGG-CGG, a rare codon in these viruses (only ~5% of arginines use CGG, and this is the only doubled CGG in the genome), and the insert contains a FauI restriction site.

nodes.label: O-76 - The PRRA furin-site insertion is out of frame relative to aligned RaTG13-MP789
nodes.title: Aligned against RaTG13 and pangolin MP789, the PRRA insertion is out of reading frame: it splits the original serine codon TCA into a new serine codon (TCT) plus part of an alanine codon (GCA).

nodes.label: O-77 - No close relative has a true S1-S2 insertion; RmYN02's reported insertion is only substitutions
nodes.title: The polybasic furin site and its PRRA insert are absent from all related lineage B betacoronaviruses; the RmYN02 virus reported to carry a natural PAA insertion at S1/S2 shows, on nucleotide alignment to its closest relatives (bat-SL-CoVZC45/ZXC21), only substitutions and no actual insertion.

nodes.label: O-10 - Early epidemic doubling time about 7.4 days and R0 estimated at 2.2
nodes.title: In the early phase the epidemic doubled in size roughly every 7.4 days, and the basic reproductive number was estimated at 2.2 (95% CI 1.4-3.9).

nodes.label: O-11 - Natural SARS-CoV-2 outbreak in farmed mink with respiratory disease, elevated mortality and interstitial pneumonia on two Dutch farms
nodes.title: In April 2020 two Dutch mink farms (NB1, 13,700 animals; NB2, 7,500 animals) had a natural SARS-CoV-2 outbreak: respiratory disease (nasal discharge to severe distress) and elevated mortality (NB1 2.4% vs 0.6% expected; NB2 1.2% vs 0.6%, 19-30 April), with severe diffuse interstitial pneumonia on necropsy in 16/18 (NB1) and 12/18 (NB2) animals.

nodes.label: O-12 - High viral loads and near-universal RT-PCR positivity across infected mink (throat swab average Ct 21.7)
nodes.title: SARS-CoV-2 RNA was detected in conchae, lung, throat and rectal swabs of all seven mink sampled for histology; across 36 necropsied animals all 36 throat swabs and 34/36 rectal swabs were positive, with average Ct 21.7 (throat) and 31.2 (rectal), indicating high respiratory viral loads.

nodes.label: O-13 - Mink-derived genomes closely related to human SARS-CoV-2; each farm's viruses cluster separately (22 nt apart), indicating two independent introductions
nodes.title: Whole-genome sequences from the mink (11 genomes across the two farms) were closely related to human-derived SARS-CoV-2 isolates; index sequences carried 9 (NB1) and 15 (NB2) substitutions vs Wuhan-Hu-1, the two farms diverged at 22 positions, and each farm's sequences clustered together, indicating separate introductions to each farm with within-farm diversity.

nodes.label: O-14 - SARS-CoV-2 RNA detected in airborne inhalable dust inside the mink sheds
nodes.title: SARS-CoV-2 RNA was detected in airborne inhalable dust sampled inside the mink sheds during the acute outbreak (e.g. NB1 house A 2/3 samples positive at Ct 35.95 and 38.18; single positives in NB1 house B and NB2), becoming negative on later sampling after the outbreak subsided.

nodes.label: O-15 - Farm workers had COVID-19-compatible illness before and around the mink outbreak on both farms
nodes.title: On both farms workers had COVID-19-compatible illness around the outbreak: at NB2 a worker was diagnosed with SARS-CoV-2 and hospitalised on 31 March (before the 19-20 April mink outbreak); at NB1 four persons had Covid-19-compatible respiratory symptoms from early April, and one NB1 worker was diagnosed on 28 April.

nodes.label: O-16 - 74 of 923 Huanan market environmental samples tested positive for SARS-CoV-2
nodes.title: Of 923 environmental samples collected at the Huanan Seafood Market from 1 January 2020 (after market closure), 74 tested positive for SARS-CoV-2 (70 by RT-qPCR and 4 additionally by Bowtie2 read-mapping); three live viruses were isolated, sharing 99.99-100% identity with the human reference isolate.

nodes.label: O-24 - The 1976 Yambuku Ebola outbreak had 318 cases and 280 deaths, index case five days after a hospital chloroquine injection
nodes.title: The first recognized Ebola outbreak comprised 318 cases and 280 deaths (case-fatality about 88%) between 1 September and 24 October 1976 in Bumba Zone, Zaire, within about 70 km of Yambuku Mission Hospital; the index case's symptom onset (1 September) came five days after he received a chloroquine injection at the hospital's outpatient clinic, and the disease was previously unknown to the region.

nodes.label: O-3 - The 155-case December-2019 dataset is incomplete and coarse (vs ~257 documented cases, no onset dates, shared addresses)
nodes.title: The early-case dataset Worobey analysed comprises 155 December-2019 cases, whereas Demaneuf (2022) documented ~257 December-2019 cases in the peer-reviewed literature; it records no symptom-onset dates, includes 7 cases sharing a single address (treated as distinct), and its coordinates were extracted with up to ~50 m of positional noise.

nodes.label: O-34 - DEFUSE was a $14.2M EcoHealth-Daszak proposal to DARPA's PREEMPT program submitted 27 March 2018
nodes.title: The 75-page 'Project DEFUSE: Defusing the Threat of Bat-borne Coronaviruses' proposal was submitted by EcoHealth Alliance (PI Peter Daszak) to DARPA's PREEMPT program on 27 March 2018, requesting $14,209,245 across 3.5 project years.

nodes.label: O-45 - No pangolins or bats were among the animals traded at the surveyed Wuhan markets
nodes.title: Across 47,381 animals surveyed over 2.5 pre-pandemic years, no pangolins and no bats were among the species traded at any of the four Wuhan markets.

nodes.label: O-46 - Animals were sold alive in stacked cages in poor condition, with on-site butchering common
nodes.title: Almost all animals were sold alive, caged and stacked in poor hygiene and welfare conditions, and most vendors offered on-site butchering.

nodes.label: O-55 - No SARS-CoV-like virus in 1,107 farmed palm civets across 25 farms in 12 provinces
nodes.title: None of 1,107 farm-raised palm civets sampled across 25 farms in 12 Chinese provinces tested positive for SARS-CoV-like virus (0/1,107).

nodes.label: O-56 - Same owner's civets negative on the farm but positive at the market
nodes.title: One farmer's 169 palm civets held on his Henan farm all tested negative, whereas his 17 civets relocated to the Xinyuan market all tested positive - the same source animals were positive only at the market.

nodes.label: O-63 - RaTG13 is ~96% genome-identical to SARS-CoV-2 but its RBD diverges
nodes.title: The bat coronavirus RaTG13 (from Rhinolophus affinis) is ~96% identical to SARS-CoV-2 across the whole genome - the closest known relative - but its RBD diverges and is predicted not to bind human ACE2 efficiently.

nodes.label: O-8 - The first four reported Wuhan cases (identified 29 Dec 2019) were all Huanan-market-linked
nodes.title: The first four cases reported through the pneumonia-of-unknown-etiology surveillance (identified 29 December 2019) were all linked to the Huanan Seafood Wholesale Market.

nodes.label: O-9 - Mean incubation period of the novel coronavirus estimated at 5.2 days
nodes.title: The mean incubation period was estimated at 5.2 days (95% CI 4.1-7.0), with the 95th percentile of the distribution at 12.5 days.
